# 01 — Repo Inventory (both repos)

Two independent principal-architect inventories of RP (split flagship / conversion+backend) and one of DD. Read-only; every claim cites file paths.

---

# PART A — DD ("rocketplate-daily-delights") — Repository Inventory

Base path: `C:/Users/dreon/AppData/Local/Temp/claude/C--Users-dreon-RocketPlate-Build/95e37d46-0c21-492a-b360-686432cf6d1a/scratchpad/rocketplate-daily-delights` (abbreviated `DD/` below).

---

## 1. Stack + build system

- **Framework**: TanStack Start (SSR React meta-framework) — `@tanstack/react-start ^1.168.26`, `@tanstack/react-router ^1.170.16`, file-based routing via `@tanstack/router-plugin`. React 19.2, TypeScript 5.8 strict, Zod 3, TanStack Query 5 (provider wired, no queries used), Tailwind CSS **v4** (`@tailwindcss/vite`), shadcn/Radix UI suite, `lucide-react`, `sonner` toasts, `lenis` smooth scroll.
- **Build**: Vite 8 via Lovable's wrapper `@lovable.dev/vite-tanstack-config@2.7.0` (`DD/vite.config.ts`), which bundles tanstackStart, viteReact, tailwind, tsconfig paths, and **nitro (build-only, Cloudflare as default target)**, plus Lovable dev-bridge plugins. Server entry redirected to `src/server.ts` (SSR error wrapper). Package name is still the template default `tanstack_start_ts` (`DD/package.json`).
- **Package manager**: Bun (`bun.lock`, `bunfig.toml` with a 24h supply-chain guard: `minimumReleaseAge = 86400`, Lovable packages excluded).
- **tsconfig** (`DD/tsconfig.json`): ES2022, bundler resolution, strict, `@/* → ./src/*`.
- **Deploy**: **No Netlify/Vercel/wrangler config files exist anywhere in the repo.** Deployment is implicitly Lovable Cloud hosting (`.lovable/project.json` → template `tanstack_start_ts_current`, project id `00c4ca86-7701-4763-b2e1-4f75564d2702`; assets served from Lovable's `/__l5e/assets-v1/...` CDN paths). `.gitignore` has Wrangler/Cloudflare entries (nitro's default target). Nothing in-repo ties it to the production RocketPlate Netlify sites.
- **Secrets**: `DD/.env` is **committed** with `SUPABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID`, and the anon/publishable JWT (anon-only — acceptable but the file is not gitignored).

## 2. Routes / pages

Three route files in `DD/src/routes/` (routeTree.gen.ts confirms exactly `/` and `/company`):

| Route | File | Purpose | Sections | Completeness |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `/` | `src/routes/index.tsx` (843 lines) | **Waitlist landing site** ("gorocketplate.com" persona — footer links to `mailto:hello@gorocketplate.com` and to `https://www.rocketplate.io` as "Main site"). | Nav, Hero (HeroVideo w/ SVG rocket + launch animation), TrustStrip marquee, PainSection (4 pain cards), HowItWorks (3 steps), TheOffer (Basic $84.99/wk 5 meals, Premium $124.99/wk 7 meals, Founding 100 offer), NoFees comparison table, MealShowcase, ForRestaurants, ForDrivers, ForInvestors, ZipSection (33186), FAQ (6 items), FinalCTA, Footer | Fully built, polished, launch-ready copy. All CTAs open `WaitlistDialog`. Canonical + og:url set. |
| `/company` | `src/routes/company.tsx` (603 lines) | **Corporate/brand landing** ("rocketplate.io" persona — JSON-LD Organization schema with `url: https://rocketplate.io`). | Nav, CinematicHero (poster w/ video drop-in slot), Problem, Model (3 steps), ForSubscribers (6 perks), ForRestaurants (6 benefits), LaunchMap (ZIP rows 33186/33176/33156/33157), Founding100, Rhythm timeline, PartnerPath (5 steps), grouped FAQ (Subscriber/Restaurant/Launch), FinalCTA, Footer (links back to `/` "Waitlist site →") | Fully built. Richer SEO head (title, og, twitter, ld+json). Hero video sources commented as "drop-in when Higgsfield/Kling/Seedance render is ready". |
| root | `src/routes/__root.tsx` | App shell: full meta/OG/Twitter head, Google Fonts (Fraunces + Inter), favicon, 404 component, error boundary reporting to Lovable (`reportLovableError`), QueryClientProvider + Outlet. | — | Complete. |

`src/router.tsx` — trivial `getRouter()` with QueryClient context, scrollRestoration.

## 3. Custom components (`src/components/*.tsx`, excluding `ui/`)

- **`WaitlistDialog.tsx`** (55 lines) — Dialog wrapper around `LeadForm`. Props: `trigger`, `defaultRole` (new enum) or legacy `defaultSegment` (mapped via `SEGMENT_TO_ROLE`: customer/restaurant/driver/investor/press/other → 5-value `Role`), `title`, `description`. Used by every CTA on both pages. Clean back-compat design.
- **`LeadForm.tsx`** (351 lines) — the core conversion form. Role select, name/email/phone/ZIP/neighborhood, live **ZIP classifier preview** (via `classifyZip`), customer-only fields (plan, household type/size, timeline, cuisine chips, dietary, Founding-100 checkbox), restaurant-only referral field, email consent (required) + SMS consent, Zod validation client-side, `submitLead()` on submit, duplicate-email handled as success. `SuccessState` shows classification badge + viral share block (native share, copy link, X, Facebook) with a **cosmetic** referral URL `/?ref=<firstName>` (not the DB-generated `referral_code` — the real code is never returned to the client since inserts can't be read back under RLS). Quality: high; minor gap — phone is `required` in markup but optional in the schema.
- **`CinematicHero.tsx`** (163 lines) — `/company` hero. Poster image (Lovable asset) always the LCP; optional `videoSrc`/`mobileVideoSrc` (mp4/webm) fades in on `canplay`; respects `prefers-reduced-motion`; staged entrance animations (badge → h1 → sub → CTAs → scroll cue); no layout shift (100svh). Well-engineered.
- **`HeroVideo.tsx`** (46 lines) — `/` hero visual. Tries `/assets/rocketplate-hero.webm|.mp4` + poster `/assets/rocketplate-hero-poster.png` — **none of these files exist in `public/`**, so `onError` flips to the always-rendered SVG `RocketPlate` fallback (graceful, but the video path is dead weight until assets ship).
- **`RocketPlate.tsx`** (100 lines) — pure SVG/CSS animated rocket-on-a-plate illustration with steam, flame, hover vs. `launching` launch animation. No deps.
- **`ScrollProgress.tsx`** (38 lines) — fixed 2px sunset-gradient scroll progress bar; mounts client-only to avoid hydration mismatch.
- **`ui/`** — 46 shadcn boilerplate files (accordion → tooltip), not customized.

## 4. Hooks + libs

**Hooks (`src/hooks/`)**
- `useSmoothScroll.ts` — Lenis smooth scrolling (lerp 0.09, duration 1.15), dynamically imported, disabled for reduced-motion and touch devices; intercepts `#anchor` clicks with −72px offset.
- `useReveal.ts` — IntersectionObserver reveal system for `[data-reveal]` / `[data-reveal-stagger]` (90ms cascade), fires once, reduced-motion shows everything immediately.
- `use-mobile.tsx` — standard shadcn `useIsMobile` (768px), unused by app code.

**Libs (`src/lib/`)**
- **`utm.ts`** — attribution capture: reads `utm_source/medium/campaign`, `v`/`variant` (landing variant), `ref`/`referred_by`, plus `document.referrer`; persists to `sessionStorage` key `rp_attribution_v1`; **first-touch wins for UTMs, latest wins for referral code**; SSR-safe. `captureAttribution()` called on mount of both routes; `readAttribution()` consumed at submit time.
- **`leads.ts`** — the live submission path. Enums (5 roles, 3 plans, 7 households, 3 timelines, 9 cuisines), `leadSchema` (Zod), `classifyZip` (priority = `{33186}`; nearby = 9 Miami-Dade ZIPs — mirrors the DB `launch_zones` seed), and `submitLead()`: builds payload with attribution + `source_domain` + `user_agent`, **direct `supabase.from("leads").insert(...)`**; on unique-violation (23505) attempts a `.update().eq("email", ...)` fallback and reports success. A comment block marks planned Resend email hooks ("fire from a `createServerFn` on insert") — not implemented.
- **`waitlist.ts`** — **legacy/dead code**: `submitWaitlist()` inserts into `waitlist_signups` with `as never` casts; imported nowhere else in `src/` (grep confirms). Superseded by `leads.ts`.
- `error-capture.ts` / `error-page.ts` / `lovable-error-reporting.ts` — SSR error stack capture (5s TTL), static 500 HTML page, and browser-side reporting into `window.__lovableEvents.captureException`.
- `utils.ts` — shadcn `cn()`.

## 5. Supabase integration (critical)

**Project**: ref `bnqxickbbsunlmuierkc`, URL `https://bnqxickbbsunlmuierkc.supabase.co` — **a completely different Supabase project from the production RocketPlate.Build project (`uhmyjevtfrynunxfsyof`)**. Confirmed in `DD/.env` and `supabase/config.toml`. `client.ts` itself reads env vars (`VITE_SUPABASE_URL` / `SUPABASE_URL`), no hardcoded ref; lazy Proxy singleton; localStorage session persistence.

**Server clients** (all Lovable-generated, `src/integrations/supabase/`):
- `client.server.ts` — service-role admin client (bypasses RLS), env `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, for server functions only. **Not imported by any app code.**
- `auth-attacher.ts` — client middleware attaching the Supabase session bearer token to serverFn RPCs; registered globally in `src/start.ts`.
- `auth-middleware.ts` — `requireSupabaseAuth` server middleware validating bearer JWT via `auth.getClaims`. **Not used by any route/function.**
- `types.ts` (603 lines) — generated `Database` types covering all tables below + `launch_zone_stats` view + `has_role` function.

**How the frontend writes**: **direct anon table INSERT into `public.leads`** (and a direct UPDATE fallback on duplicates) from the browser — no RPC, no server function. This directly violates the parent repo's ADR-002 "RPC-only public writes" rule; there is also **no rate limiting** anywhere.

**Migrations (`supabase/migrations/`, all 2026-07-07):**

1. **`20260707211007…`** — `waitlist_segment` enum; **`waitlist_signups`** table (id, segment, email, name, zip, phone, restaurant_name, cuisine, notes, referrer, user_agent, created_at); indexes on segment/created; GRANT INSERT to anon+authenticated; RLS enabled; policy **"Anyone can join waitlist"** — INSERT to anon/authenticated WITH CHECK on email regex + per-field length caps. No SELECT policy (write-only from client).
2. **`20260707213135…`** — 4 lead enums (`lead_role`, `lead_plan`, `lead_household`, `lead_timeline`); **`leads`** table (~30 columns: identity, zip/neighborhood, role/plan/household/cuisines[]/dietary/household_size/timeline/founding_100_interest, email+sms consent, referral_source/referred_by/restaurant_referral, launch_zone_status, notes, utm_source/medium/campaign, landing_page_variant, user_agent) with 18 CHECK constraints (email regex + length caps on every text field); indexes zip/role/created_at; GRANT INSERT anon+authenticated, ALL to service_role; RLS on; policy **"Anyone can join the RocketPlate waitlist"** — INSERT `WITH CHECK (true)`; explicitly no SELECT/UPDATE/DELETE policies.
3. **`20260707213155…`** — replaces that INSERT policy with a stricter WITH CHECK re-validating email format + all length bounds inline.
4. **`20260707222144…`** (largest) —
   - `app_role` enum + **`user_roles`** table; `has_role(uuid, app_role)` SECURITY DEFINER function; policies "Users can view own roles" (SELECT, `auth.uid() = user_id`) and "Admins manage roles" (ALL, `has_role(auth.uid(),'admin')`).
   - `leads` extensions: `updated_at`, **`referral_code` (unique, auto-generated by BEFORE INSERT trigger `generate_referral_code` — 8-hex from `gen_random_bytes`)**, `referral_count`, `source_domain`; **unique index on `lower(email)`** (this is what makes the duplicate-update path fire); `update_updated_at_column()` trigger; admin policies on leads: "Admins can view leads" (SELECT), "Admins can update leads" (UPDATE), "Admins can delete leads" (DELETE) — all gated on `has_role(...,'admin')`.
   - **`launch_zones`** table (zip_code unique, city, neighborhood, state='FL', `launch_zone_status` enum waitlist/nearby/priority/active/paused, priority_score, target_launch_date) — policies: "Anyone can view launch zones" (SELECT anon+authenticated USING true), "Admins manage launch zones" (ALL admin). Seeded with 10 Miami ZIPs (33186 priority @100, nine "nearby").
   - **`restaurant_partner_applications`** table (business/contact/email/phone/cuisine/address/zip/website/instagram/pos_system/locations/can_batch_2_to_4_pm/estimated_capacity/status enum new→onboarded/notes/utm/source_domain, all CHECK-constrained) — "Anyone can apply to partner" (INSERT WITH CHECK true) + 3 admin policies. **No frontend form writes to this table** (the restaurant path funnels into `leads` with role `restaurant_owner`).
   - **`launch_zone_stats`** view (`security_invoker = on`): per-zone `real_lead_count` + `restaurant_partner_count`; SELECT granted to anon — but since the underlying `leads` SELECT requires admin under invoker rights, anon reads of the counts will fail. **Nothing in the frontend reads it.**
   - **`email_events`** table (lead_id FK cascade, event_type, sent_at, status, provider_message_id, metadata jsonb) — admin SELECT only; unused by the app.
5. **`20260707222209…`** — hardening: REVOKE execute on trigger helpers from PUBLIC/anon/authenticated; `has_role` execute revoked from anon, granted to authenticated only.

**Notable data-layer issue**: `submitLead`'s duplicate branch attempts an anon `UPDATE ... WHERE email = ...`, but there is **no anon UPDATE policy on `leads`** (only admin) — the update silently affects 0 rows while the UI still tells the user "We updated your preferences." Also `email_consent`/`sms_consent`, referral counting, and email hooks have no server-side machinery.

## 6. SSR / server pieces

- **`src/server.ts`** — custom Nitro server entry wrapping TanStack's `server-entry`: catches thrown SSR errors and also detects h3-"swallowed" 500 JSON bodies (`{"unhandled":true,"message":"HTTPError"}`), recovering the real stack from `lib/error-capture.ts` and rendering the static `lib/error-page.ts` HTML.
- **`src/start.ts`** — `createStart` registering `attachSupabaseAuth` (global function middleware) and an error-normalizing request middleware.
- **`client.server.ts` / `auth-middleware.ts`** — service-role client and JWT-validating middleware, both **scaffolded but unused** (no `createServerFn` exists in the codebase; grep for server functions finds none). All meaningful work happens client-side.

## 7. Assets

- `src/assets/` — 3 committed JPGs: `hot-meals-hero.jpg` (four dinner plates), `chef-kitchen.jpg`, `doorstep-delivery.jpg`; plus 2 **Lovable CDN asset manifests** (not local files): `rocketplate-logo.svg.asset.json` (238KB SVG, served from `/__l5e/assets-v1/...`) and `hero-cinematic-poster.jpg.asset.json` (232KB, the `/company` hero poster — "silver rocket at sunset"). These CDN URLs only resolve on Lovable hosting.
- `public/` — **only `favicon.ico`.** The `/assets/rocketplate-hero.{mp4,webm}` + poster referenced by `HeroVideo.tsx` do not exist; no video anywhere in the repo.

## 8. Docs

- **`AGENTS.md`** — Lovable-injected boilerplate only: warns not to rewrite pushed git history because the branch two-way syncs with the Lovable editor.
- **`INTERACTION_DIRECTION.md`** — a genuinely good motion-design spec: "directed product film" principles (weight/intent/warmth/restraint), a 4-easing vocabulary, Lenis scroll rules, a 13-step section pacing script, the `data-reveal` system contract, a microinteraction table, depth/3D policy ("No Three.js yet — cost/benefit doesn't clear the bar for a waitlist site"), mobile and reduced-motion rules. The implemented hooks/CSS follow it closely.
- **`src/routes/README.md`** — TanStack Start file-routing conventions cheat-sheet (index/dynamic `$`/optional/splat/layout), warns against Next.js/Remix directory conventions; `routeTree.gen.ts` is generated.

## 9. Git history arc

46 commits, **2026-07-01 → 2026-07-07** (one week), single branch `main`-style. Arc: `template: tanstack_start_ts_current-6f0053eadbb1` → ~10 anonymous Lovable "Changes"/"Work in progress" commits → **"Built RocketPlate waitlist site"** (the `/` page) → "Added RocketPlate motion props" → **"Added RocketPlate leads table"** (migration 2) → **"Built rocketplate.io landing"** (the `/company` page) → "Prepped cinematic hero for video" → "Applied copy deck to site" → HEAD **"Added lead & admin RLS"** (migrations 4–5). Roughly two-thirds of commits are Lovable auto-messages ("Changes"), consistent with iterative prompt-driven building; the 5 named commits mark the real milestones. No merges, no tags, no deploy-config commits.

---

### Key architect-level takeaways

1. **Two parallel backends**: DD writes to Supabase project `bnqxickbbsunlmuierkc`; the production RocketPlate.Build stack uses `uhmyjevtfrynunxfsyof`. Any leads captured here live in a separate database.
2. **Write path violates the parent constitution**: browser-direct anon INSERT (and a broken silent UPDATE) on `leads`, vs. ADR-002's RPC-only + rate-limited rule. Schema-level CHECKs + RLS WITH CHECK are solid, but there is no rate limiting or captcha.
3. **Overlap/duplication**: `waitlist.ts` + `waitlist_signups` are a dead legacy path; `restaurant_partner_applications`, `email_events`, `launch_zone_stats`, `user_roles` admin machinery exist in the DB with no frontend or server code using them.
4. **Frontend quality is high** (SSR-safe hooks, reduced-motion discipline, LCP-aware hero, documented motion system); it is essentially two complete landing pages plus a sophisticated lead form, with hero-video and email-automation slots stubbed for later.

---

# PART B — RocketPlate (RP) — Flagship Pages Inventory (read-only audit)

Repo: `C:\Users\dreon\RocketPlate.Build` — static multi-page vanilla HTML/CSS/JS, two Netlify sites (flagship `rocketplate` deployed from repo root; conversion site `gorocketplate` deployed from `gorocketplate/` with its own `netlify.toml`). All pages are single-file (inline CSS + inline JS); the only shared stylesheet is `dash.css`. Backend is Supabase project `uhmyjevtfrynunxfsyof`, called exclusively through PostgREST RPCs with the publishable key (`sb_publishable_wsUbTQXWP3dYHyJDbhOmEQ_LSEPOZfO`, hardcoded in every page that talks to the backend).

---

## 1. Per-page inventory

### 1.1 `index.html` — flagship landing (1,928 lines)

**Purpose:** Pre-launch waitlist landing for subscribers (plus restaurant/driver/investor segmentation). "Cinematic 3D launch site" per its own header comment (lines 1200–1205).

**Structure:** head/meta lines 1–36; inline CSS 37–698; markup 700–1194; JS 1199–1926.

**SEO meta (lines 13–33):** `<title>` (13), meta description (14), canonical `https://www.rocketplate.io/` (15), OG title/description/type/url/image (16–20), `twitter:card summary_large_image` (21), JSON-LD `Organization` with `areaServed: Miami-Dade County, FL` and `sameAs` gorocketplate.com (22–33). Guarded GTM stub at lines 7–11 (no-op until `GTM-XXXXXXX` is replaced). Note: **no `<link rel="icon">` on this page**.

**Third-party CDN libs:**
- GSAP 3.12.5 + ScrollTrigger — cdnjs (1196–1197)
- Lenis 1.1.14 smooth scroll — jsdelivr (1198)
- Three.js r128 — cdnjs, injected lazily, desktop-only (1746–1754)
- Google Fonts: Fraunces / Outfit / IBM Plex Mono (34–36)

**Accessibility:** skip-link (702, styled 170–174); burger with `aria-expanded`/`aria-controls` + Escape-to-close (721–723, 1243–1254); segment buttons get `aria-pressed` (1763–1769); form status region `role="status"` (1142); ZIP console result `role="status" aria-live="polite"` (1080); decorative canvas/ticker/SVG are `aria-hidden` (706, 779, 987, 1055); descriptive `alt` text on food photos (882–885); labeled inputs with autocomplete attributes (1126–1129); `prefers-reduced-motion` kills reveals, ticker, steam, Lenis/GSAP, and freezes the rocket (192–195, 285, 296, 1207, 1298, 1700).

**Forms→RPCs:** see deep map §2 (waitlist → `upsert_lead`; ZIP console → `zip_wait_count`).

### 1.2 `partners/index.html` — restaurant partner page (545 lines)

**Purpose:** B2B marketing + partner application + tokenized application-status tracker. Three mutually exclusive views: `#marketing` (default, 220–414), `#statusView` (token present, 205–215), `#done` (post-submit, 417–428).

**Section map (marketing view):**
| Section | Lines | Content |
|---|---|---|
| `#hero` | 223–248 | "Your slowest hours…revenue lane" + dark "control card" (counts locked 10 PM, batch 2–4 PM, one pickup) |
| `#deadzone` | 251–275 | Dead-zone opportunity copy + CSS bar chart of a kitchen's day |
| `#how` | 278–292 | 6 op-cards: why batch beats marketplace chaos |
| `#rhythm` | 295–307 | 5-stop daily clock timeline |
| `#split` | 310–342 | Dark band: "RocketPlate handles" vs "Restaurants provide" |
| `#fit` | 345–359 | 6 best-fit criteria checklist |
| `#apply` | 362–402 | 15-field application form |
| `#final` | 405–412 | Final CTA |

**JS behaviors (438–543):** `rpc()` fetch helper to `POST {SB.url}/rest/v1/rpc/<fn>` (450–457). Submit → `submit_partner_application` with `{p: app}` (529) where `app` (519–527) = `business_name, contact_name, email, phone, cuisine_type, address, zip_code, website, instagram, pos_system, number_of_locations, can_batch_2_to_4_pm, estimated_capacity, best_time_to_contact, notes`. Response `out.access_token` builds the private status link `?token=` (530–531). With `?token=`, `boot()` calls `get_my_application {p_token}` (497) and renders a 5-stage pipeline (`new → reviewing → tasting → menu_setup → live`, STAGES 444–448) plus `declined`/`on_hold` banners (474–484). Rate-limit message on `rate_limited` (535–536). HTML-escapes all rendered values via `esc()` (441). Motion: IntersectionObserver `.rv → .in` reveals (461–464) — no GSAP/Lenis/Three here.

**SEO:** full title/description/canonical/OG/twitter (13–20). **Broken refs:** `<link rel="icon" href="../assets/logo.png">` (21) and header/footer `<img src="../assets/logo.png">` (200, 432) — `assets/logo.png` does not exist (logo lives at repo root `logo.png`), so the partners page favicon and both logo images 404 on the flagship site. Header/footer links also hardcode `https://rocketplate.netlify.app/` (200, 432, 434).

**Accessibility:** required-field markers, `aria-invalid` styling + focus on first bad field (514–516), `role="alert"` on form msg (397), reduced-motion honored (50, 442), readonly status-link input labeled (423).

### 1.3 `partner/index.html` — legacy redirect stub (16 lines)

`noindex`; JS `location.replace("/partners/" + location.search)` preserving `?token`, plus meta-refresh fallback (5–11). Server-side 301 lives in `netlify.toml` (7–10).

### 1.4 `admin/index.html` — internal metrics dashboard (194 lines) — see §3

### 1.5 `account/index.html` — lead self-service profile (176 lines) — see §3

### 1.6 `privacy/index.html` (39 lines) & `terms/index.html` (39 lines)

Plain-language pre-launch legal pages styled by `dash.css`. Privacy: collect/use/don't/email-SMS/choices sections (25–35). Terms: free waitlist, Founding 100 offer terms, no-service-promise, restaurant-application-not-contract (25–35). Both carry `meta name="robots" content="noindex"` (line 8 in each) **yet are listed in `sitemap.xml` (lines 6–7)** — an internal contradiction worth resolving. Both also reference the nonexistent `../assets/logo.png` favicon (line 9); their visible brand img uses `../logo.png`, which is fine.

### 1.7 `contact/index.html` (34 lines)

Routing page: 4 stat-card links (waitlist / partners / driver / investor-press), notes direct inbox comes with the mail domain at launch (30). Has title+description (6–7), indexable (no robots meta), in sitemap. Same broken `../assets/logo.png` favicon (8).

### 1.8 `dash.css` (73 lines)

Shared design system for admin/account/privacy/terms/contact: brand tokens (2–8), `.gate` auth card (21–23), form fields (24–27), buttons (28–34), `.stat-grid`/`.stat` (38–43), `.panel` (45–47), `.bars` horizontal bar rows (49–54), `.tbl` tables + status `.tag` variants (56–64), `.chip-group` checkbox chips using `:has(input:checked)` (65–68), `.row2`, `.loading`.

### 1.9 `robots.txt`, `sitemap.xml`, `netlify.toml`

- `robots.txt`: allow all, `Disallow: /admin/` + `/account/`, sitemap at www.rocketplate.io (1–5).
- `sitemap.xml`: 5 URLs — `/`, `/partners/`, `/contact/`, `/privacy/`, `/terms/` with priorities 1.0/0.8/0.3/0.2/0.2.
- `netlify.toml`: 301 `/partner/* → /partners/:splat` (7–10); global security headers `nosniff`, `X-Frame-Options: DENY`, `Referrer-Policy`, `Permissions-Policy` (12–18); `/assets/*` `max-age=31536000, immutable` (22–25); `logo.png` 7-day cache (27–30).

### 1.10 `.netlify/`, `.claude/launch.json`, `.github/`

- `.netlify/state.json`: flagship `siteId: fa64163e-5ea6-4062-95ce-e7fe87f519a9`. (`gorocketplate/.netlify/` exists for the second site.)
- `.claude/launch.json`: dev server = `python -m http.server 8734` (matches CLAUDE.md Windows quirk: `python`, not `python3`; note local http.server can't serve Range requests, hence "verify on real deploy").
- `.github/workflows/`: `smoke.yml` and `supabase-keepalive.yml` (tracked; CI runs the smoke test and keeps Supabase warm).

## 2. `index.html` deep map

### Section-by-section (`<section>`/landmark ids)

| Id | Lines | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `header#hdr` + `#mobileMenu` | 708–734 | Fixed nav; 6 anchors + Partner + Join CTAs; burger menu |
| `#hero` | 739–776 | H1 "Dinner, cleared for launch", CTAs, hero-note, hero-media video slot, steam/meal-chip placeholder FX, scroll cue |
| `.ticker` | 779–787 | Rotated navy marquee (track duplicated at 1289–1290 for a seamless loop) |
| `#why` | 790–815 | Problem framing: 4 pain-point chips + animated delivery-app "receipt" ($13.50 → $26.92 fee stack) vs "≈$17 a plate" chip |
| `#how` | 818–849 | 3-step model (restaurants cook / RP batches / subscribers eat) + cutoff strip |
| `#subscribers` | 852–869 | 6 benefit cards + CTA |
| `#kitchens` | 872–895 | 4 labeled food photos + cuisine chips; "dishes representative, partners revealed at launch" disclaimer (878 — honors the no-fake-data rule) |
| `#pricing` (contains `#founding` banner) | 898–949 | Founding 100 perks banner (904–916); Basic $84.99/wk (5 dinners) vs Premium $124.99/wk (7 dinners) plans (918–947) |
| `#restaurants` | 952–1003 | B2B pitch, 6 chips, 3 numbered points, navy "dead-zone window" bar card (labeled illustrative, 986) |
| `#rhythm` | 1006–1018 | 4-stop daily timeline (9 PM cutoff → 10 PM counts → 2–4 PM prep → 4:15–6:30 delivery) |
| `#path` | 1021–1036 | 5-step partner path (mirrors partner-app pipeline stages) |
| `#launch` | 1039–1084 | Launch cities copy + "mission control" SVG map (pilot beacon ZIP 33186) + ZIP-check console form `#zipConsole` |
| `#faq` | 1087–1107 | 9 `<details>` items in 3 audience groups |
| `#join` | 1110–1147 | Waitlist card: 4-way segment control, progress bar, 4 fields, 2 consents, submit |
| `#launchpad` | 1150–1160 | Final CTA pair |
| `footer` | 1166–1194 | Brand, Explore/Company link columns (incl. legal routes), © line |

### Three.js rocket system (`initRocket`, 1437–1743)

- Fixed full-viewport `canvas#stage` at `z-index:5, pointer-events:none` (69–74, 706).
- **Procedural toy rocket, zero external 3D assets:** lathe-geometry body (1475–1482) and crimson nose (1485–1492), torus collar (1495–1498), porthole ring+glass+glint (1501–1518), 3 extruded fins (1527–1541), nozzle stack (1544–1549), layered cone flame + PointLight (1552–1571), **signature orbiting dinner plate with cloche dome** (1573–1596), 12-puff smoke pad (1599–1613).
- **Scroll choreography:** keyframe tables `KF_DESKTOP` (1619–1634) and `KF_MOBILE` (1635–1650), each row `[progress, x, y, rotY turns, tiltZ, scale, flame, smoke]` mapped to page sections (comments name each beat: hero pad → liftoff → per-section fly-bys → centered nose-up over the join form → footer drift). `sample()` lerps between keyframes with ease-in-out (1653–1667); scroll progress is damped `curP += (targetP-curP)*0.07` (1699); mouse parallax desktop-only (1679–1685).
- **Living details:** idle bob/sway (1703–1704), flame flicker plus a *scroll-velocity throttle* — fast scrolling lengthens the exhaust via `window.__scrollVel` fed by Lenis (1297, 1309–1312, 1712–1717); smoke only visible at the pad (1724–1737).
- **Loading policy:** three.js (~600KB) injected on `requestIdleCallback` (timeout 2500ms), desktop-only; mobile gets the poster instead (1744–1754). Reduced-motion pins the rocket to keyframe 0 (1700).

### Lenis / GSAP usage (1292–1434)

Guarded by `!prefersReduced && window.gsap` (1298); else all `.rv` elements are force-shown (1431–1434). Lenis: `duration 1.15`, custom exponential ease, driven by `gsap.ticker`, `lagSmoothing(0)`; anchor clicks glide with `-76px` header offset (1304–1324). Motion tiers per INTERACTION-DIRECTION.md: (1) every `main h2` split into word spans (`splitWords`, 1328–1346) staggered 0.045s with `expo.out` (1347–1354); hero load-in timeline eyebrow→h1 words→lede→CTAs→note→cue (1357–1369); (2) 11 named card groups stagger in as one beat (1372–1384); (3) generic `.rv` reveals (1387–1392). Special moments: receipt lines slide in + fee total counts $13.50→$26.92 from `data-base`/`data-total` (1394–1408); join-card lock-in (1411–1414); background wash parallax scrub (1417–1420); sticky mobile CTA visibility window that avoids overlapping the form (1423–1430).

### Hero video slot mechanics (743–757, 1256–1286)

`<video muted loop playsinline preload="none" poster=…>` with webm+mp4 sources (744–747). Runtime: desktop + motion-ok → HEAD-request `assets/rocketplate-hero.mp4`; if 200, add `.has-video`, flip `preload="auto"`, `load()`+`play()`, and hand the hero stage to the film by fading `#stage` (the 3D canvas) to opacity 0 until scrolled past `0.55 * innerHeight` (1265–1278). Mobile/reduced-motion → HEAD-check the poster and add `.has-poster` (1280–1285). If neither asset exists, the CSS steam + drifting meal-chip placeholder stays (749–756, 226–266) — video "drops in via /assets, no code changes" (comment 740–742).

### Waitlist form → exact RPC (1756–1925)

Constants: `SUPABASE.url/anonKey` (1215–1218), `VARIANT = "rocketplate-io-v1"` (1219), `ZONES` priority `["33186"]` + 12 nearby ZIPs (1220–1225), `ROLE_MAP` subscriber→`customer`, restaurant→`restaurant_owner`, driver→`driver_operator`, investor_press→`investor_press` (1227), UTM/ref capture from query params (1228–1234), `rpTrack` pushes `rp_*` events to `dataLayer` (1235–1236).

Submit (1838–1924): client validation (names/email regex/zip, required consent) then
`POST {url}/rest/v1/rpc/upsert_lead` with headers `apikey` + `Authorization: Bearer <anonKey>` and body `{ p: { … } }` (1860–1879). Payload fields (1867–1879): `first_name, last_name, email` (lowercased), `zip_code, role, cuisine_interests: [], founding_100_interest` (checkbox AND role===customer), `email_consent: true, launch_zone_status` (from `zoneFor`), `referred_by` (`?ref=`), `referral_code` (client-random 8-char base36), `utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign, user_agent` (trimmed 400), `landing_page_variant: "rocketplate-io-v1"`, `source_domain: location.hostname`.
Responses: `rate_limited` → friendly cooldown message (1884–1888); `status:"updated"` → "already on the list" (1892–1897); create → zone-specific copy + optional `access_token` link to `https://rocketplate.netlify.app/account?token=…` (1899–1904), GSAP mini rocket-launch on the button arrow (1908–1916).

ZIP console (1804–1836): local zone classification + `POST /rest/v1/rpc/zip_wait_count` with `{p_zip}`; if a positive number returns, appends "N NEIGHBORS WAITING" (real aggregate counts only, per comment 1804); pre-fills the waitlist ZIP field (1835).

---

## 3. Admin & Account — auth model and data flows

**Common pattern:** no Supabase Auth, no cookies. Both are static pages calling SECURITY DEFINER RPCs with a bearer *capability token* passed as an RPC argument; the publishable anon key only authenticates the PostgREST layer. Both are `noindex, nofollow` (admin line 6; account line 6) and Disallowed in `robots.txt` (3–4).

### `admin/index.html`
- **Auth:** operator pastes an admin token (`rp_…`) into a password field (16–28); stored in `sessionStorage["rp_admin"]` (80, 181–184); every RPC sends it as `p_token`; an `unauthorized` error clears the session and re-gates (154–158); "Lock" button clears it (187).
- **Reads (no writes):**
  - `admin_dashboard {p_token}` (150) → renders `totals` (leads / founding_interest / sms_opt_in / last_7d / partner_apps, 105–110), `by_zone`, `by_role`, `cuisine`, `by_variant` bar charts (112–117), `by_zip` table (119–122), `zones` seeded launch-zone table with priority_score/lead & partner counts/target date (124–128), `partners` applications table (130–136), `recent_leads` latest-50 table incl. plan/zone/F100/referrals/source (138–143).
  - `admin_export_leads {p_token}` and `admin_export_partners {p_token}` (188–189) → client-side CSV builder + blob download (164–177).
- All rendered strings HTML-escaped via `esc()` (79).

### `account/index.html`
- **Auth:** per-lead access token from `?token=` in the emailed private link (92); no token → "open from your link" gate (16–21, 129).
- **Reads:** `get_my_lead {p_token}` (131, re-fetched after save at 159) → greeting, plan/ZIP-zone/Founding-100 stat cards (106–110), pre-fills all preference inputs incl. `cuisine_interests[]` checkboxes (111–121), referral link `https://rocketplate.netlify.app/?ref=<referral_code>` + `referral_count` (122–125).
- **Writes:** `update_my_lead {p_token, p: prefs}` (158) with `prefs` (144–156) = `interested_plan, household_type, zip_code, phone, neighborhood, feeding_count, dietary_preferences, subscribe_timeline, founding_100_interest, sms_consent, cuisine_interests[]`.
- `SITE` constant still points at `rocketplate.netlify.app` with a comment to swap when DNS is live (90).

Both conform to ADR-002's RPC-only write path — no page anywhere does a direct table read/write; `Grep` confirms every backend call goes through `/rest/v1/rpc/` (index 1823/1860; partners 451; admin 83; account 97; smoke 39).

---

---

## 5. `scripts/smoke.mjs` — what it verifies (88 lines, zero-dependency)

Run `node scripts/smoke.mjs`; exit 1 on any failure. Four blocks:
1. **Flagship routes** (58–67): GET 200 for `/`, `/partners/`, `/privacy/`, `/terms/`, `/contact/`, `/admin/`, `robots.txt`, `sitemap.xml`, `assets/rocketplate-hero.webm`; plus `/partner/x` must return **301** (67) proving the Netlify redirect.
2. **Go site routes** (70–72): `/`, `robots.txt`, hero webm on `gorocketplate.netlify.app`.
3. **Security headers** (75–77): `x-content-type-options: nosniff` on both sites; `cache-control: immutable` on the flagship poster asset.
4. **Backend** (80–81): `zip_wait_count {p_zip:"33186"}` RPC must return a JSON `number`; Supabase Edge Function health check `GET /functions/v1/send-emails?selftest=1` must be 200 (the Resend email worker's self-test).

Targets the Netlify URLs, not the custom domains (9–10), matching the "Netlify canonical until Gate C" ADR-003.

---

## Notable audit observations (for the caller)

1. **Broken `../assets/logo.png`** on partners/privacy/terms/contact (paths above) — visible logo 404s on `/partners/`.
2. **`noindex` vs sitemap contradiction:** privacy/terms are `noindex` (line 8 each) yet listed in `sitemap.xml` (6–7).
3. **Hardcoded `*.netlify.app` URLs** in user-facing copy: index 1143 (gorocketplate.netlify.app), 1903 (account link), partners 200/432/434, account 20/30/90 — all pending the DNS cutover flag at account line 90.
4. **GTM placeholder** (`GTM-XXXXXXX`) still unset on both flagship pages (index 7, partners 7) — `rpTrack` events currently go to a dead `dataLayer`.
5. **Flagship hero mp4 is 16.6 MB** vs the go site's 5.7 MB; mitigated by webm-first sources, `preload="none"`, HEAD-gated desktop-only activation, and immutable caching.
6. Security posture is consistent: RPC-only writes, publishable key only, capability tokens as RPC params, HTML-escaping on all dynamic renders, `nosniff`/`DENY`/referrer/permissions headers site-wide.

---

# PART C — RocketPlate — Conversion Site + Backend Docs Inventory (read-only audit)

Repo: `C:/Users/dreon/RocketPlate.Build` (RP). Audited 2026-07-19 at `f33ab3d`.

---

## 1. `gorocketplate/index.html` — full map (1,249 lines, single-file site)

Static HTML, inline CSS/JS. External deps: Google Fonts (Fraunces/Outfit/IBM Plex Mono), CDN GSAP 3.12.5 + ScrollTrigger, Lenis 1.1.14. No three.js on this site.

### Sections (in DOM order)

| # | Section | ID | Content |
|---|---|---|---|
| — | Header | `#hdr` | Logo + single "Join the Founding 100" CTA (`data-track="header_cta"`); blur+shadow on scroll |
| 1 | Hero | `#hero` | Video slot (`#heroMedia`/`#heroVideo`: webm+mp4 sources, poster `assets/rocketplate-hero-poster.jpg`) with animated fallback (gradient atmo, 5 steam wisps, bobbing SVG rocket with pulsing flame). H1 "Hot local meals. One weekly subscription. Zero delivery chaos." Dual CTA + micro-line + quick capture form. Video activates only if desktop, motion-OK, and a `HEAD` fetch of the mp4 succeeds (lines 874–887) |
| 2 | Pain | `#pain` | 6 pain cards: delivery fees, tip pressure, cold food, random prices, too many decisions, no weekly rhythm |
| 3 | Meet RocketPlate | `#how` | **3** step cards (choose plan / pick menus / evening route 4:15–6:30 PM) |
| 4 | Founding 100 | `#founding` | 4 offer cards (first week free / 33% off month two / 12-month rate lock / early access) + CTA |
| 5 | ZIP checker | `#zipcheck` | `#zipForm` → zone verdict + real count (below) |
| 6 | Cuisine orbit | `#cuisines` | 8 cuisine satellites JS-positioned on a circle (line 890–899), CSS counter-rotation 46s, pause on hover; reduced motion → flat grid. Copy: "Partner names are revealed at launch" |
| 7 | Partner teaser | `#partners` | Navy card; CTA links off-site to `https://rocketplate.netlify.app/partners/` (`data-track="partner_cta"`) |
| 8 | FAQ | `#faq` | 8 `<details>` items (delivery-app diff, hot meals, windows, launch areas, swaps, no fees/tips, Founding 100, restaurants) |
| 9 | Lead form | `#join` → `#leadForm` | Full capture (below) + success state `#successWrap` |
| 10 | Final CTA | `#launchpad` | "Bring RocketPlate to My Area" (`data-track="final_cta"`) |
| — | Sticky mobile CTA | `.m-cta` | ≤960px; ScrollTrigger shows from 0.9×viewport until 1.4×viewport before page end (hides at form); non-GSAP fallback just shows it |
| — | Footer | | Brand blurb, anchors, cross-links to `rocketplate.netlify.app` (main site, contact, privacy, terms) |

### Lead form (`#leadForm`) — every field

Required: `first_name`, `last_name`, `email`, `phone`, `zip_code`, `role` (select: customer / restaurant_owner / driver_operator / investor_press / other), `interested_plan` (radio chips: 5_meals / 7_meals / not_sure, default not_sure), `household_type` (individual / couple / family / office / student / senior / other), `cuisine` checkboxes (cuban_latin, bbq, italian, mexican, asian_fusion, burgers, seafood, healthy_light, surprise_me — ≥1 required), `email_consent` checkbox (required).
Optional (inside `<details class="optional">`): `neighborhood`, `dietary_preferences`, `feeding_count` (1/2/3-4/5+), `subscribe_timeline` (immediately / within_month / within_3_months / just_curious), `referral_source` (friend_family / social_media / search / press / flyer_local / other).
Other consents: `wants_founding_100` (checked default), `sms_consent` (optional, TCPA-worded).
UX: gradient progress bar `#formProg` driven by 7 required checks (line 1061–1074), per-field `.ferr` errors + `aria-invalid`, disabled button "Reserving your spot…", rate-limit-specific error copy.

### RPC payload (lines 1100–1130) — `POST {SUPABASE.url}/rest/v1/rpc/upsert_lead`, body `{ p: lead }`

`first_name, last_name, email (lowercased), phone, zip_code, neighborhood, role, interested_plan, household_type, cuisine_interests[], dietary_preferences, feeding_count, subscribe_timeline, founding_100_interest, sms_consent, email_consent, source_domain (location.hostname), referral_source, referred_by (?ref param), referral_code (client-generated 8-char), launch_zone_status (client-computed zone), utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign, user_agent (400 chars), landing_page_variant: "gorocketplate-v1"`.
Auth: publishable key `sb_publishable_wsUbTQXWP3dYHyJDbhOmEQ_LSEPOZfO` as `apikey` + `Bearer`; Supabase url `https://uhmyjevtfrynunxfsyof.supabase.co`. Response: `{ status: 'created'|'updated', referral_code, access_token }`; `rate_limited` in the error message triggers cooldown copy.

### Quick capture (`#quickForm`, hero)

ZIP + email inputs; **no network call** — prefills `#f-zip`/`#f-email`, fires `rp_quick_capture_submit {has_zip, has_email}`, Lenis-scrolls to `#join`, focuses first name. Note text: "we'll carry these over."

### ZIP checker

Client `ZONES` config: `priority: ["33186"]`, `nearby:` 12 Kendall/Pinecrest ZIPs (33143…33196). On submit: regex `^\d{3,10}$`, zone verdict copy (priority / nearby / not_yet), then real count via `POST /rest/v1/rpc/zip_wait_count` `{ p_zip }` — shown only when > 0 ("N neighbors are already waiting in ZIP"). Result includes a "Join for {zip}" button and prefills the form ZIP. Tracks `rp_zip_check` and `rp_zip_result {zip, zone}`.

### Success / referral state (`#successWrap`)

- Duplicate path: headline swaps to "You're already on the launch list… updated your preferences."
- `#acctLink` → `https://rocketplate.netlify.app/account?token=<access_token>` (unhidden only if RPC returned a token).
- Zone card `#sZone`: zone copy + fresh `zip_wait_count` ("You're one of N…").
- Referral: `https://www.gorocketplate.com/?ref=<referral_code>` in `#refLink`, copy button (clipboard w/ execCommand fallback), share links to X / WhatsApp / Facebook / mailto with encoded pitch text.
- Restaurant referral (`#restoRefForm`): name input → `POST /rest/v1/rpc/refer_restaurant` with `{ p: { restaurant_name, zip, referrer_email } }` (zip/email pulled from the lead form fields); tracks `rp_restaurant_referred`.

### Analytics hooks

- Guarded GTM loader in `<head>` (lines 7–11): no-op until `GTM_ID` placeholder is replaced (regex-validated).
- `rpTrack(event, props)` → `window.dataLayer.push({event: "rp_"+name, ...props, variant: "gorocketplate-v1"})`.
- Delegated click tracking for every `[data-track]`: header_cta, hero_cta, hero_secondary, quick_capture, founding_cta, zip_check, zip_join, partner_cta, form_submit, sticky_cta, final_cta, copy_referral, share_x/whatsapp/facebook/email, restaurant_referral.
- Programmatic events: `rp_quick_capture_submit`, `rp_zip_result`, `rp_lead_created` / `rp_lead_updated` `{zone, role, plan}`, `rp_success_shown` `{zone, duplicate}`, `rp_restaurant_referred`.
- UTM + `?ref` captured from query string at load.

### SEO meta

Title + meta description (waitlist-focused), canonical `https://www.gorocketplate.com/`, OG title/description/type/url/image (`assets/og.jpg`), `twitter:card summary_large_image`, favicon `assets/logo.png`, font preconnects. **No JSON-LD on this site.** `robots.txt` allows all + sitemap pointer; `sitemap.xml` lists only `/`. `netlify.toml`: nosniff, X-Frame-Options DENY, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, immutable 1-year cache on `/assets/*`.

---

## 2. Backend as documented (BACKEND.md + LAUNCH-REVIEW.md)

Supabase project `uhmyjevtfrynunxfsyof` (us-east-1, org "A SaaS Nation"), shared by both sites.

### Tables (7 per delta report; 6 detailed in BACKEND.md + `restaurant_referrals` in BUILD-NOTES/LAUNCH-REVIEW)

| Table | Purpose / notes |
|---|---|
| `leads` | Full capture + `updated_at` (auto-touch trigger), `referral_count`, `source_domain`, `founding_100_interest` (renamed from `wants_founding_100`), `feeding_count`, `subscribe_timeline`, `access_token`. Unique on `lower(email)`. No anon INSERT — writes only via `upsert_lead` |
| `launch_zones` | Public marketing data (anon SELECT): zip, city, neighborhood, status (priority/nearby/future/live), priority_score, target_launch_date. Seeded 33186 + 12 nearby. No count columns (private totals stay in admin views) |
| `restaurant_partner_applications` | Full partner intake incl. pos_system, can_batch_2_to_4_pm, capacity, `status` pipeline (new→reviewing→tasting→menu_setup→live / declined / on_hold), `best_time_to_contact`, `access_token`. No public policies; writes via `submit_partner_application` |
| `email_events` | Resend queue: `lead_id` OR `application_id`, `event_type` (7 types), status, sent_at, provider_message_id, metadata. No policies = service-role only |
| `restaurant_referrals` | Success-state suggestions (go site). Post-hardening: writes via `refer_restaurant` RPC only |
| `submission_log` | Rate-limit ledger (ip, tbl, created_at). Service-role only |
| `admin_credentials` | One row, bcrypt token hash (rotate SQL in BACKEND.md:85) |
| (`waitlist`) | Implied by migration `create_waitlist_table` — legacy first table, not documented as active |

### RPCs (all SECURITY DEFINER)

Public writes: `upsert_lead(p jsonb)` (validates email/name/ZIP/consent server-side; INSERT … ON CONFLICT lower(email) DO UPDATE of preference columns only; first-touch attribution never overwritten; returns status/referral_code/launch_zone_status/access_token), `submit_partner_application(jsonb)`, `refer_restaurant(p jsonb)` (added by `harden_public_write_paths`, 2026-07-19).
Public read: `zip_wait_count(p_zip)` (integer aggregate, only intentional public count).
Token-gated per-record: `get_my_lead(token)`, `update_my_lead(token, prefs)`, `get_my_application(token)`.
Admin (bcrypt-token): `is_admin`, `admin_dashboard(token)`, `admin_export_leads(token)`, `admin_export_partners(token)`.
(Delta report counts "14+ RPCs" verified via SQL — LAUNCH-REVIEW.md:87-89 lists the names.)

### Triggers

- `rate_limit_guard` BEFORE INSERT on leads, partner apps, referrals — reads `x-forwarded-for`, 10 submissions / 10 min / IP, raises `rate_limited`; non-HTTP contexts exempt (BACKEND.md:52-55).
- `queue_lead_emails` on lead insert (not on update — no re-queue spam). Event mapping (BUILD-NOTES:51-59): waitlist_confirmation (every signup), founding100_nurture (customer + founding interest), restaurant_partner_confirmation (role=restaurant_owner), area_nurture (not_yet zone); campaign-driven: zip_launch_announcement, card_on_file_invite, launch_conversion.
- `set_updated_at` auto-touch on leads (search_path pinned in hardening batch).

### Views (service-role / definer only)

`admin_leads_by_zip`, `admin_cuisine_demand`, `admin_zone_stats` — consumed by `admin_dashboard`, not granted to anon/authenticated.

### Edge function

`send-emails` — deployed, ACTIVE, dormant (no `RESEND_API_KEY`): drains queued `email_events`, renders HTML templates for all 7 event types, marks sent/failed. Auth = service-role Bearer; unauthenticated `GET ?selftest=1` health check; auto dry-run while key unset. Activation is founder work (Resend domain + 3 secrets + cron) — BACKEND.md:110-131.

### Migrations

BACKEND.md:134 lists only 4 (`create_waitlist_table → create_leads_and_email_events → relax_leads_for_light_capture → lead_platform_upgrade`) then names 2 more mid-doc (`dashboards_backend`, `admin_rpcs_and_upsert_token`). LAUNCH-REVIEW.md:90-93 gives the fuller ordered list of 9: those 6 + `partner_best_time_to_contact` + `partner_application_email_events` (+ edge fn v2) — listing 8 names while saying "9". Plus `harden_public_write_paths` applied 2026-07-19 (LAUNCH-REVIEW.md:120). **BACKEND.md's migration list is stale/incomplete.**

### Security model claims

RLS on every table; **zero** anon INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE policies anywhere post-hardening (ADR-002); public surface = SELECT on `launch_zones` + EXECUTE on the validated RPCs; no public read of people data; server-side validation in RPC; trigger-level IP rate limiting; duplicate-graceful upsert preserving first-touch attribution; no service-role key client-side (grep-verified per delta). Verified 2026-07-19: raw inserts rejected 42501, RPC 200, advisors WARNs cleared except intended architecture.

---

## 3. Docs corpus — summaries + freshness

| Doc | Summary | Freshness |
|---|---|---|

| `CLAUDE.md` | Engineering constitution: 8 hard rules (RPC-only writes, no client secrets, migrations-only DDL, verify on real deploy, no fake data, docs move with code, performance, Windows quirks) + War Room/Phase Zero framing + key refs. | **Current** (dated 2026-07-18 directive; reflects hardening) |

The living operational truth doc. | **Current** — most items in the 07-16 banner are superseded by their own strikethroughs below (banner items 1, 2 partially stale by design; issue tracker is up to date) |

| `INTERACTION-DIRECTION.md` | Shared motion system: tokens (eases, staggers, durations), Lenis config, 3-tier reveal system, 3D policy (three.js only for the flagship rocket), section pacing, microinteractions, reduced-motion contract, perf budget. | **Current**; go-site code matches tokens (Lenis 1.15, expo/power3, 0.045 word stagger). Minor: doc says `-76px` header offset, go site uses `-72` |

---

## 4. ADRs (`docs/adr/`, all Accepted 2026-07-18)

| ADR | Decision |
|---|---|
| ADR-001 static-first-until-gate-c | Stay dependency-light static HTML (no framework/bundler) until Gate C (paid subscriptions), then adopt a framework + re-evaluate hosting in one move. Tolerates copy duplication across the two sites |
| ADR-002 rpc-only-write-path | All public writes via validated SECURITY DEFINER RPCs (`upsert_lead`, `submit_partner_application`, `refer_restaurant`); raw anon write policies dropped (`harden_public_write_paths`); reads via per-record access-token or bcrypt-admin RPCs. Token patterns → Supabase Auth + RBAC before Gate C |
| ADR-003 netlify-canonical-until-gate-c | Reject the V2-directed Vercel migration during the static phase; Netlify stays canonical with deploy-history rollback; platform question reopens at Gate C |
| ADR-004 two-site-variant-strategy | Keep flagship (brand) + go (conversion) split through Phase Zero as a deliberate variant test into one `leads` table segmented by `landing_page_variant`/`source_domain`; revisit at DNS cutover or a >2x conversion winner |

---

## 5. LAUNCH-REVIEW: known issues + checklist state

**Known issues (as of 2026-07-18/19):** 0 deploy blocker RESOLVED (Netlify Personal, both sites live; `//proxy/` URL gotcha); 1 three.js-on-mobile FIXED `883fc29`; 2 webm encodes FIXED `e13ca4f` (go hero 5.7MB→0.4MB); 3 Supabase auto-pause restored + keep-alive workflow added (active only once repo is pushed); 4 write-path hardening APPLIED 2026-07-19; **5 OPEN** GitHub backup blocked (stale Dreone6 credential — repo still has no pushed remote = single-laptop risk); **6 OPEN** minor hero-film text softness; **7 OPEN** legal pages are placeholders, TCPA review required pre-paid-traffic/SMS; **8 OPEN** constructed receipt figures ($13.50→$26.92) on flagship to replace day-of-launch; **9 OPEN** cross-site links on *.netlify.app until DNS cutover; **10** local python preview can't serve Range requests (env quirk).

**Launch checklist (all 7 unchecked, all founder-gated):** DNS both domains → HTTPS; run domain swap + redeploy; Resend domain/secrets/cron (+dryRun test); GTM ID in 3 files; rotate admin token (the one pasted in chat 2026-07-07 — still pending per delta §0.5-G); one real signup per site verified end-to-end; formal privacy/terms + TCPA sign-off.

---

## 6. Contradictions: docs vs what `gorocketplate/index.html` actually does

1. **Stale in-file comment** — `index.html:826` says "Backend: Supabase (RLS: anon INSERT only; counts via RPC only)" but the code below it uses **RPC-only writes** (`upsert_lead`, `refer_restaurant`). The comment describes the pre-hardening architecture and now contradicts ADR-002 and the code in the same file.
2. **BUILD-NOTES DB section is pre-hardening** — `gorocketplate/BUILD-NOTES.md:38-47` claims leads "RLS: anon INSERT only… duplicate submit returns 409" and restaurant_referrals "Anon INSERT only". Reality (BACKEND.md, code): no anon INSERTs anywhere; duplicates return `{status:'updated'}` from `upsert_lead` (no 409 path in code); referrals go through `refer_restaurant`. Also uses old column name `wants_founding_100` — the payload sends `founding_100_interest` (index.html:1117).
3. **Dead prefill hook / wrong claim** — BUILD-NOTES:18 says the partner teaser "CTA prefills role=restaurant_owner". The `data-role-prefill` handler exists (index.html:1045-1049) but **no element in the file carries that attribute** (grep-confirmed); the partner CTA (line 563) navigates off-site to `/partners/` instead. Handler is dead code; the FAQ's "pick 'Restaurant Owner'" advice is the only in-page path.
4. **Step count** — BUILD-NOTES:14 says "#how | 4-step system cards"; the page has 3 steps (index.html:491-496).
5. **Asset names** — BUILD-NOTES says drop `rocketplate-hero-poster.png` and replace `assets/og.png`; the actual/referenced files are `.jpg` (`rocketplate-hero-poster.jpg`, `og.jpg` — both exist, so the checklist item "Replace og.png" is stale-done). Checklist items for hero video and privacy/terms are also stale-unchecked (video files exist in `gorocketplate/assets/`; legal placeholders deployed per LAUNCH-REVIEW).
6. **Event name drift** — BUILD-NOTES:76 lists a `lead_duplicate` analytics event; the code fires `rp_lead_updated` (index.html:1159). DNS-AND-ANALYTICS.md:104 has the correct `rp_lead_updated`.
7. **JSON-LD overstatement** — LAUNCH-REVIEW.md:74-76 groups "JSON-LD Organization" under SEO checks for public pages; the go site has none (flagship-only).
8. **Client-computed zone/referral values** — the browser sends `launch_zone_status`, `referral_code`, and `source_domain` in the payload (index.html:1120-1124), i.e. client-supplied even though `upsert_lead` returns its own `referral_code`/`launch_zone_status` (BACKEND.md:63). Not a doc contradiction per se, but the client `ZONES` list and the DB `launch_zones` seed are duplicated config that must be kept in sync manually (BUILD-NOTES:80-82 acknowledges only the client side).
9. **BACKEND.md migration list incomplete** — lists 4 migrations at line 134 vs 9-10 actual (LAUNCH-REVIEW.md:90-93 + `harden_public_write_paths`); LAUNCH-REVIEW itself says "9" while naming 8. Violates constitution rule 6 mildly (docs lag DDL).
10. **INTERACTION-DIRECTION offset** — doc specifies `-76px` anchor offset; go site uses `-72` (index.html:916, 1027, 1039). Cosmetic.

Key paths: `C:/Users/dreon/RocketPlate.Build/gorocketplate/index.html`, `gorocketplate/BUILD-NOTES.md`, `gorocketplate/netlify.toml`, `gorocketplate/robots.txt`, `gorocketplate/sitemap.xml`, `BACKEND.md`, `LAUNCH-REVIEW.md`, `README.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `COPY-DECK.md`, `DESIGN-DIRECTIONS.md`, `INTERACTION-DIRECTION.md`, `DNS-AND-ANALYTICS.md`, `ROCKETPLATE_CURRENT_STATE_DELTA.md`, `docs/adr/ADR-001…004`.
